image.alt: Island Orthodontics team and patients sharing bright smiles

paragraphs.text: Welcome to Island Orthodontics, a respected orthodontic practice serving Deer Park, Ronkonkoma, Roslyn and nearby Long Island communities. We focus on delivering high-quality, personalized care in a friendly, comfortable environment using modern tools like Invisalign and digital treatment planning.
Our team treats children, teens and adults with tailored plans, flexible payment options, and convenient scheduling so your path to a healthy, confident smile is smooth and straightforward.

items.title: Personalized Care
items.text: Multiple specialists and experienced doctors collaborate to create treatment plans tailored to each patient’s needs.

items.title: Advanced Options
items.text: We offer Invisalign<sup>®</sup> clear aligners, Empower self‑ligating braces, and adjuncts like PROPEL to optimize treatment and comfort.

items.title: Easy Scheduling
items.text: Request appointments online, call or text our office, and choose flexible payment plans to fit your family’s schedule.

items.title: Local Recognition
items.text: An award‑recognized local practice with an emphasis on patient experience and community care.

paragraphs.text: Dr. Efraim Zak, DDS, is a specialist in orthodontics who trained at New York University College of Dentistry, completing both his DDS and orthodontic residency there. He brings extensive clinical and research experience and continues advancing his skills through seminars and study groups.

Dr. Renée Nykolak, DMD, MS, is committed to high‑quality orthodontic care and uses modern technologies to create comfortable treatment experiences. She completed her DMD at the University of Pennsylvania and specialty training in Orthodontics and Dentofacial Orthopedics at the University of Detroit Mercy.

Dr. Devin Zolnowski, DMD, completed dental training at Rutgers School of Dental Medicine and specialty orthodontic training at Maimonides Medical Center, where he served as Chief Resident. He brings a patient‑centered approach and strong clinical training to every case.

Dr. Bronwyn McAndrews, DDS, has decades of orthodontic experience and a conservative, patient‑focused approach. She trained at the University of Southern California School of Dentistry and completed orthodontic residency training at the University of Connecticut.

Dr. Michael Kirshblum, DMD, MS, completed his DMD at the University of Pennsylvania and advanced orthodontic training with a Master of Science from Temple University. He combines rigorous training with a patient‑centered approach to care.

Dr. Kruno Tovilo, DDS, completed dental training at Boston University and postgraduate orthodontic studies at New York University. He brings broad clinical experience treating children and complex adult cases and participates in education and lecturing.

Dr. Adam D. Shatz, DDS, completed his DDS and orthodontic residency at SUNY Stony Brook and brings over a decade of specialty experience. He maintains active involvement in professional organizations and continuing education.

Dr. Upasna Malhotra, DDS, completed dental training and specialty orthodontic training at Stony Brook University and Montefiore Medical Center. She focuses on delivering an enjoyable, patient‑focused treatment experience using current orthodontic technology.

Dr. Mike Cotzas, DDS, completed his dental degree at Columbia University and orthodontic training at Montefiore Medical Center. He remains active in continuing education to bring current techniques to patient care.

Dr. Matthew Miller, DDS, completed specialty orthodontic training at NYU and has published on orthodontic topics including prevention of white spots. He brings a research‑informed approach and modern clinical techniques to patient care.

Dr. Princy Kuriakose, MS, DDS, completed advanced orthodontic training at the University of Washington and maintains Board‑level practice standards. She combines clinical research experience with a compassionate approach to patient care.
We offer clear aligners, advanced bracket options, retainers and adjuncts to create tailored treatment plans for children, teens and adults.

Get Started.title: What to expect at your first visit

paragraphs.text: Your first visit begins with a friendly check-in and a conversation about your smile goals. We’ll perform a complete oral exam, take any needed X-rays, and review your dental and medical history so we can recommend the right treatment options for you.

Bring your driver’s license, insurance card, and a list of current medications. Our team will walk you through next steps, answer questions about timing and cost, and help you choose a plan that fits your lifestyle.
We accept most major insurance plans and file claims electronically to make visits easier. If you’re unsure whether we accept your plan, call our office and we’ll verify benefits for you.

Flexible financing options and interest-free plans may be available to help fit treatment into your budget. We also offer a complimentary consultation so you can learn about options before committing to treatment.
Save time at your first appointment by completing forms online for your location, or download and print the appropriate PDF. If you have questions about a form, our team is happy to help.

image.alt: Smiling patients of all ages at an orthodontic practice

paragraphs.text: Traditional metal braces are the most common kind of braces, and they are now more comfortable than ever. Metal braces employ steel brackets and archwires to straighten your teeth. To produce a unique and colorful smile, colored elastics (rubber bands) may be placed on metal braces.

image.alt: Two stylish smiling teens taking selfie with braces

paragraphs.text: All self-ligating braces are made of the same materials as standard braces. Because self-ligating braces do not use elastics, they often need fewer adjustment visits and can reduce friction on the teeth. We offer self-ligating options in metal, ceramic, and translucent brackets.

image.alt: Close-up of braces and orthodontic archwire

paragraphs.text: Ceramic braces are made of translucent materials that blend with your tooth color for a less noticeable option. They are commonly chosen by older adolescents and adults who prefer a discreet look. Ceramic brackets are effective but require careful oral hygiene to protect their appearance.

image.alt: Smiling person wearing clear ceramic braces

paragraphs.text: Invisalign<sup>®</sup> clear aligners are removable, nearly invisible trays that move teeth using advanced 3D imaging for a precise fit. They let you brush, floss, and eat without the restrictions of brackets and wires, offering a comfortable option for many adults and teens.
SmartTrack® materials and SmartForce features help control tooth movement so a wide range of issues — including crowding, spacing, and some bite problems — can be addressed with aligners.

image.alt: Close-up of clear aligners being placed on teeth

paragraphs.text: Lingual braces are attached to the back surfaces of the teeth so they are hidden when you smile. Each set is custom-made to fit your mouth, making lingual braces a discreet option for performers, athletes, and anyone seeking an invisible fixed appliance.

image.alt: Smiling family enjoying time together at dental office

paragraphs.text: TADs are small titanium anchors used in select orthodontic cases to provide additional support and speed certain tooth movements. They are placed comfortably under local anesthesia and removed once they have served their purpose.

image.alt: 3D treatment planning software showing orthodontic simulation

paragraphs.text: Two-phase treatment begins early to guide jaw growth and correct serious bite problems while permanent teeth are still erupting. The second phase typically finishes tooth alignment with comprehensive braces once most permanent teeth are present, helping achieve stable, long-term results.

image.alt: Young patient receiving early orthodontic intervention

paragraphs.text: Maintain your new smile with custom retainers designed for comfort and long-term stability. We offer both removable and fixed options, and we provide guidance to help your results last a lifetime.

image.alt: Young woman smiling and holding orthodontic retainer

image.alt: Patient smiling with orthodontic braces in our Emeryville office

paragraphs.text: Invisalign transparent aligners are used to correct teeth using a clear plastic aligner system.

accordion.header: Is Invisalign a suitable fit for my hectic schedule?

paragraphs.text: Absolutely! Invisalign integrates seamlessly with your lifestyle because they are removable and invisible. With Invisalign, you can continue to do all the things you did before orthodontic treatment. Treatment appointments with Invisalign may be planned to fit into your everyday schedule. A brief progress check is carried out every six weeks.

accordion.header: When can I remove my aligners?

paragraphs.text: In general, you should wear your aligners for 22 hours per day, removing them only to eat, brush, and floss. However, you can also remove them for a special night out on the town occasionally.

accordion.header: What exactly is the Invisalign system, and how does it work?

paragraphs.text: Straightening your teeth and getting the smile you want is possible because of the nearly invisible Invisalign<sup>®</sup> system. Your treatment plan is mapped with 3D computer imaging, showing the movement from your starting position to the planned result. A customized series of clear aligners is made for you to gradually shift your teeth. You’ll typically wear each aligner for about two weeks before moving to the next one, and your orthodontist will tailor the total treatment time to your individual needs.

accordion.header: Do Invisalign’s key advantages outweigh the drawbacks?

paragraphs.text: Many patients find the benefits outweigh any drawbacks. Invisalign aligners are removable and nearly invisible, so they fit smoothly into daily life. You can eat the foods you enjoy, keep your normal oral hygiene routine, and typically spend less time in the office compared with traditional braces. Invisalign also offers a digital preview of your expected outcome, which helps you visualize the result before treatment begins.

accordion.header: How many Invisalign patients are there?

paragraphs.text: Over 5 million people worldwide have used Invisalign to straighten their teeth. If you’d like to learn whether Invisalign is a good fit for you, we’ll be glad to answer your questions and outline a personalized plan at your consultation.
